programmes.show_description: Britain's sharpest armchair critics watch Wild Isles, Rise and Fall, Challenge Anneka, Race Across the World and The Great Celebrity Bake Off for Stand Up To Cancer.
Brand new series: The weddings erupt into chaos when one groom forgets the rings, while bride Gia frets that a big secret could send her groom Scott running...
Computer programmer Andrew has been single for 10 years. Will he click with old-fashioned romantic Sarah? And Kenny and his biceps 'James' and 'Andrew' return to the restaurant.
Please Homer Don't Hammer 'Em: Marge discovers that she has a gift for carpentry, but has to use Homer as a cover in order to overcome prejudices against women builders.
Han Ukkah Solo: When Beverly talks Erica into writing a Hanukkah song for the school holiday concert, she inspires Barry to prove his own musical prowess.
O Captain! My Captain!: Beverly hopes to inspire the students when she takes over as substitute teacher in Barry's chemistry class. Adam embarrasses Emmy.
Chris threw a woodland, fairy wedding for Corinne in 2012. Now he chats with bridesmaid Sam and best man Alex to answer tough questions about the big day.
Dillon leans on Lucas for support, but will sparks re-ignite between the former lovers? Elsewhere, a mum confides in her friends at a yoga class.
Theresa pushes Mercedes to give Warren another chance. A phone call makes Dillon concerned about Lucas's wellbeing.
A short, cheeky clip from Rude Tube's naughty little sister Rude(ish) Tube, featuring sheep.
Grasshopper Also: In Carlstadt, New Jersey, Gordon Ramsay attempts to save the Grasshopper Also, a family-owned Irish restaurant that's on its last legs.
Yippee Ki Yay Melon Farmer: Barry is thrilled about celebrating Christmas at Lainey's house. Marvin makes a big promise to Adam.
The Wedding Singer: Beverly's excited about Barry and Lainey's impending nuptials, but is the groom starting to have second thoughts?
A Game of Chicken: Gloria tries to secretly baptise the twins before Haley and Dylan get home. Cam is at war with his school's mascot. Claire deals with the closet fiasco fallout.
The Last Thanksgiving: Cameron and Mitchell's friends mistakenly assume they've split up - and they've definitely chosen a side. And Haley attempts to cook Thanksgiving dinner.
Brand new series: The weddings erupt into chaos when one groom forgets the rings, while bride Gia frets that a big secret could send her groom Scott running...
A short, cheeky clip from Rude Tube's naughty little sister Rude(ish) Tube, featuring sheep.
Cafe Tavolini: In Bridgeport, Connecticut, Gordon Ramsay visits Cafe Tavolini, a restaurant that's being neglected as the couple who run it teeter on the edge of divorce.
Zeke's: In New Orleans, Gordon Ramsay visits a restaurant famed for its Cajun food and loyal locals. But under new ownership, standards have slipped and the customers have left.
Hungry Hungry Homer: Homer goes on hunger strike when he discovers that the Springfield Isotopes baseball team are planning to move to Albuquerque.
Bye Bye Nerdy: When she gets bullied by a new girl at school, Lisa discovers that there's a 'nerd scent' that attracts bullies to nerds. So she invents an antidote.
Finale (Part 1/2): Mitchell and Cam settle on their new normal, and Phil and Claire decide that one of the kids needs to move out so they can take control of the house again.
Brand new series: The weddings erupt into chaos when one groom forgets the rings, while bride Gia frets that a big secret could send her groom Scott running...
The coach rolls into Porto, where the celebs go off-roading on dirt buggies and valuable life lessons are learned. But who will be packing their bags?
G.I. (Annoyed Grunt): Homer accidentally signs up for basic training in the army. And Marge and Grampa get roped in when they try to get him released.
Moe 'n' a Lisa: Lisa discovers Moe's poetic talent and helps get him published. And he's so successful that he - and the Simpsons - are invited to a literary conference.
A new cellmate spells danger for Lucas, forcing Dillon to take desperate measures. Diane and Tony continue to lie to protect their loved ones. A resident's romantic hopes end in betrayal.
...Australia - Brand new series: One couple are smitten on honeymoon. Another are very much not. And two new couples get hitched, including flashy English groom Danny.
Great Expectations and Rise and Fall are on the box this time. And Wild Isles, Tempting Fortune, Married at First Sight Australia and Dance 100.
The Goggleboxers watch The Great Celebrity Bake Off for SU2C, Race Across the World, Love Is Blind, The Dog Academy, Beef, Outlast and ITV News on criminal charges against Trump.
Will Ryan's unique chat-up lines laugh aspiring embalmer Kerry into bed? How will nervous Mark react when free spirit Sophia reads his palm?
A celebration of Naked Attraction's very best naughty bits, revisiting times when the show broke new dating ground, including when trans woman and celebrity Lauren Harries entered a pod.
Brand new series: One couple are smitten on honeymoon. Another are very much not. And two new couples get hitched, including flashy English groom Danny.
Great Expectations and Rise and Fall are on the box this time. And Wild Isles, Tempting Fortune, Married at First Sight Australia and Dance 100.
Treehouse of Horror XVII: Homer ingests a blob from an asteroid; Bart enslaves a clay monster; and everyone thinks that aliens are invading.
Snow Day: Adam and Barry take advantage of Murray's fear of snow. Erica isn't pleased when Beverly finds her college application.
Agassi: Adam tries to join the school tennis team to stay close to his best friend Chad, while Beverly helps Erica to get through her post-break-up depression.
Danielle and Jordan pull no punches as they relive their knockout boxing-themed wedding. Wrong kind of ring, Jordan! But has Danielle forgiven him? (Ep8)
SuperShowerBabyBowl: Jay is hosting a Super Bowl party, while Claire hosts a baby shower for Haley - and they're both at Jay's house. What could possibly go wrong?
Red Alert: Lily has reached a womanly milestone that Cam and Mitch are not at all prepared for. So they call in reinforcements...
A short, cheeky clip from Rude Tube's naughty little sister, Rude(ish) Tube, featuring sports of the lawnmower, motorbike and bicycle variety.
Cafe Tavolini: In Bridgeport, Connecticut, Gordon Ramsay visits Cafe Tavolini, a restaurant that's being neglected as the couple who run it teeter on the edge of divorce.
The Pina Colada Episode: Murray and Geoff try to pull Beverly, Barry and Erica out of their shared misery after Lainey's departure. Coach Nick returns to town.
I Coulda Been a Lawyer: Beverly campaigns for a new stop sign on the street. Barry panics about his college applications, and Murray's attempt to help makes things worse.
Tree's a Crowd: The Dunphy home is bursting when Dylan's hippie mum moves in and Claire's stepdad wants to visit. Jay and Gloria try to help Manny get over his breakup.
The Last Christmas: Cameron hopes to keep everyone happy for Christmas dinner, but Mitchell thinks he has an ulterior motive. Meanwhile, Claire tries to dodge Jay's job offer.
Brand new series: One couple are smitten on honeymoon. Another are very much not. And two new couples get hitched, including flashy English groom Danny.
La Frite: In Los Angeles, Gordon Ramsay visits La Frite, a French restaurant that's being run into the ground by its squabbling family owners.
Gordon Ramsay revisits three restaurants that he helped - Lido, Anna Vincenzo's and Le Bistro. Have the owners put his advice to good use?
Simpson Safari: The Simpsons win a holiday to Africa. But when Homer enrages a hippo, the trip soon becomes a dangerous adventure...
Trilogy of Error: Homer gets his thumb sliced off while trying to steal a brownie. Lisa misses the school bus. Bart discovers Fat Tony's stash of smuggled fireworks.
Pilot: The first episode of the Emmy-winning comedy, shot from the perspective of an unseen documentary maker. It's not easy being part of a family these days...
Brand new series: One couple are smitten on honeymoon. Another are very much not. And two new couples get hitched, including flashy English groom Danny.
In Porto, two newbies make a big impression, a port-tasting session loosens lips, the celebs learn new tricks at circus school, and one pair feel the heat.
Ice Cream of Margie (With the Light Blue Hair): Marge, feeling overshadowed by Homer's new career selling ice cream, starts creating ice lolly stick sculptures.
The Haw-Hawed Couple: Bart becomes Nelson's best friend when Marge makes him go to his birthday party. Which means no one can mess with Bart. But there are downsides too...
Treehouse of Horror XXXII: Another Halloween helping of spooky tales, featuring some chilling takes on classic films including Parasite, Nightmare on Elm Street and, er, Bambi.
...Australia - Brand new series: The experiment intensifies. Gia considers taking a big step after years of celibacy, while tensions explode in Fiji for Bec and Danny.
This time, the Goggleboxers watch In with a Shout, Gordon Ramsay's Future Food Stars, Rise and Fall, Saving Our Wild Isles, Last Woman on Earth, Obsession, and Dr. Down Below.
The Goggleboxers have been watching Britain's Got Talent, Scared of the Dark, Obsession, Family Fortunes, Love Is Blind, Inside the Factory, and BBC News (nurses' strikes).
Olive and Terence, who are both in their 80s, bond over memories of lost loves, tennis and amateur dramatics. Anita meets rugby star Max, and Frankie's date is teacher Charlie.
